title: SOCI Part 6A Protected Information — access auditing and on-disclosure offence prevention
exclusions: Excludes information that is no longer Protected Information after a defined declassification event (track in `protected_info_lifecycle_lookup`). Excludes legitimate disclosure to authorised recipients (Government, Court, ASD, named legal advisers) documented in `protected_info_authorised_disclosure_lookup`.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.52.24` archived every 15 minutes to `audit_evidence` with `soci_clause="SOCI-Part-6A"`. Every row pins (artefact_id, classification, accessor_id, action, src_ip, app, recipient_email_domain, recipient_country, evidence_artefact, first_seen, last_seen, status). Dashboard `SOCI — Part 6A Protected Information Audit` surfaces accesses and outbound transfers.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Continuously evidences (a) every access to Protected Information is logged with an identity, (b) outbound transfers (email, cloud share, USB) are detected, (c) the recipient classification (authorised / unauthorised / unknown) is captured, and (d) suspected on-disclosure offences are routed to legal counsel. This is the full scope of the Part 6A on-disclosure-offence control surface.
compliance.controlObjective: Proves the entity controls Protected Information access and disclosure consistent with the Part 6A offence regime.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search `UC-22.52.24` archived to `audit_evidence`, the `SOCI — Part 6A Protected Information Audit` dashboard, and per-event evidence-artefact URLs in the audit-evidence store.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Email a Protected Information document to an external personal email address. UC-22.52.24 MUST surface within 15 minutes with `status="unauthorised_disclosure_suspect"`.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Email the same document to a documented authorised recipient. UC-22.52.24 MUST record `status="authorised_disclosure"`.

dataSources: DLP (Microsoft Purview, Forcepoint DLP) for outbound email, cloud, USB. SharePoint Online audit log (`o365:management:activity`) for SharePoint Protected Information sites. Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps (CASB) for cloud-share detection. Email gateway logs (Mimecast / Proofpoint).
app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (Splunkbase 3110), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055), Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621).

splunkbaseApps.name: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services

spl:
((index=dlp sourcetype IN (microsoft:purview:dlp, forcepoint:dlp) classification="protected" earliest=-15m@m) OR
 (index=email sourcetype IN (mimecast:siem, ms365:email_audit) earliest=-15m@m attachment_classification="protected") OR
 (index=cloud sourcetype IN (mscas:event, splunk:mscs:gws) earliest=-15m@m item_classification="protected" event_type IN ("file_external_share","file_download")))
| eval action = case(
    sourcetype IN (microsoft:purview:dlp, forcepoint:dlp), action,
    sourcetype IN (mimecast:siem, ms365:email_audit), "email_send",
    1=1, event_type)
| eval recipient_email_domain = lower(coalesce(recipient_email_domain, mvindex(split(recipient,"@"),1)))
| eval recipient_country = coalesce(recipient_country, "unknown")
| lookup protected_info_authorised_disclosure_lookup recipient_email_domain OUTPUT authorised
| eval status = case(
    authorised="true", "authorised_disclosure",
    recipient_email_domain IN ("gmail.com","outlook.com","yahoo.com","hotmail.com","icloud.com","proton.me"), "unauthorised_disclosure_suspect_personal_email",
    isnull(recipient_email_domain), "unauthorised_disclosure_suspect_internal",
    1=1, "requires_review")
| stats earliest(_time) AS first_seen latest(_time) AS last_seen count AS event_count by artefact_id classification accessor_id action src_ip app recipient_email_domain recipient_country status
description: Detects access, outbound email, cloud-share, and USB transfer of Protected Information. Cross-references against authorised-disclosure lookup; routes suspected on-disclosure offences to legal counsel.
value: Part 6A criminalises unauthorised disclosure of Protected Information; a single missed event can be a criminal liability. This UC keeps protected-information audit always-on with high-fidelity detection and ties every event to the audit-evidence trail.
implementation: (1) Classify Protected Information at-source (Microsoft Information Protection / Forcepoint labelling). (2) Onboard DLP, email gateway, SharePoint, CASB into Splunk. (3) Maintain `protected_info_authorised_disclosure_lookup` of authorised recipient domains. (4) Schedule UC-22.52.24 every 15 minutes; route `unauthorised_disclosure_suspect_*` and `requires_review` to notable severity=high. (5) Archive every row.
visualization: (Panel 1) Live unauthorised-suspect feed. (Panel 2) Per-accessor inventory. (Panel 3) Recipient-domain distribution. (Panel 4) Country distribution.

references.title: SOCI Act 2018 (Cth) — Part 6A Protected Information — sections 41 through 47
Microsoft Information Protection / Purview — Sensitivity Labels
Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services

knownFalsePositives:
1. **Internal classification drift** — documents mislabelled as Protected; mitigation via Information Manager review.

2. **Authorised recipient using personal email** — some Government recipients use personal accounts in remote roles; pair with named-individual exception.

3. **Auto-classified false positives** — a draft document mis-classified at creation. Mitigation: align label policies with stakeholder review.

4. **Encrypted attachment**: encrypted ZIP / OneDrive with password may not surface content; ensure DLP exception or block policy.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `soci_22_52_24_exceptions`; tight max grant 4 hours with Information Security Manager + General Counsel joint sign-off.
equipmentModels: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (Splunkbase 3110)
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- Splunk Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud.
- UC-22.51.13 deployed (provides general audit-log baseline).
- ES, Microsoft 365 add-on, Microsoft Cloud Services add-on.
- `index=dlp`, `index=email`, `index=cloud`, `index=audit_evidence`.
- Document classification scheme published; Protected Information labelled at-source.
- Lookup `protected_info_authorised_disclosure_lookup` of authorised recipient domains/individuals; owned by Information Security Manager.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Enable DLP in Microsoft Purview (or Forcepoint DLP):

- Rule pattern: classification label `Protected` OR sensitivity-label `SOCI-Protected`.
- Action: alert + audit (block in stricter environments).

Onboard email-gateway logs:

```ini
[mimecast://siem]
base_url = https://eu-api.mimecast.com
log_type = siem
interval = 600
index = email
sourcetype = mimecast:siem
```

Onboard Microsoft 365 audit:

```ini
[ms365_management_activity://splunk]
tenant_id = <tenant>
content_type = Audit.SharePoint,Audit.Exchange
interval = 300
index = cloud
sourcetype = o365:management:activity
```

## Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Schedule every 15 minutes:

```ini
[UC-22.52.24: SOCI Part 6A Protected Information audit]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -15m@m
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.52.24,reg=SOCI,clause=SOCI-s46
action.notable.param.rule_title = SOCI Part 6A Protected Information suspected disclosure
action.notable.param.security_domain = audit
action.notable.param.severity = high
alert.severity = 5
```

Notable severity by `status`: `unauthorised_disclosure_suspect_*` → critical; `requires_review` → high; `authorised_disclosure` → audit-evidence only.

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a)** Positive control: from a test account, email a labelled-Protected file to a personal Gmail; UC must surface within 15 minutes.

**(b)** Negative control: same to documented authorised recipient; UC records `authorised_disclosure`.

**(c)** Coverage: validate at-source labelling on a sample of 10 Protected documents.

## Step 4 — Operationalize

**Dashboard**: `SOCI — Part 6A Protected Information Audit`.

**Runbook for `unauthorised_disclosure_suspect_*`** (owner: Information Security Manager + General Counsel, severity P1):

1. Within 30 minutes: triage. Engage General Counsel.
2. Within 24 hours: if confirmed: notify CISC under Part 6A obligation; consider mandatory-breach notification under the Privacy Act.
3. Document the disposition.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting

**Labelling drift**: ensure documents are auto-labelled on creation or labelled by the Information Manager.

**Encrypted attachments**: implement policy to deny encrypted outbound emails containing Protected labels.

**Internal SharePoint sharing**: `file_external_share` to unauthorised users is also surfaced.

**SOCI-specific failure modes**:

- **Cross-jurisdictional sharing**: a multinational entity may legitimately share Protected information internationally; consider data-residency restriction policy.
- **Government recipient mismatch**: a Government recipient using a personal email address; require named individual exception with formal documentation.
- **MSSP access**: an MSSP analyst legitimately accessing Protected as part of incident response; tracked separately in `protected_info_authorised_access_lookup`.
- **Legal-privilege protection**: communications with named legal counsel are usually privileged; document the disclosure even though it is authorised.
grandmaExplanation: Some pieces of information about our critical-infrastructure asset are 'Protected' — the law makes it a crime to share them with the wrong people. We watch every email, cloud share, and USB transfer of those documents. If we see one going to a personal email, an unknown address or an unknown country, we ring the bell and call the lawyers.
